human_verification.notes: Verified issue #1476 requirements, source coverage, network service assurance boundary, hold/release behavior, confidentiality and approval handling, scenario family coverage, and scenario-to-ability mappings for the validated spec pack.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit quality status, hold/release, defect, incident, corrective-action, refusal, and escalation behavior.
Required abilities across shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and professional-and-information-services sets are fully covered and mapped.

specialist_owned_evidence: Evidence-backed network service assurance is constrained to tenant service model, SLA/SLO definitions, tickets, incidents, problems, alarms, telemetry, service tests, SAT records, quality checks, approvals, communication logs, and corrective-action evidence.
Hold, release, nonconformance, repeat-defect, incident, confidentiality, customer workflow, and approval-conflict behavior is explicit and routed to correct adjacent owners.

Engineering, NOC command, security, legal, editorial, regulatory, financial, service-credit, contractual, formal QA, customer-approval, and professional-judgment boundaries trigger escalation instead of overconfident closure.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because tenant-specific source precedence, jurisdictional duties, service commitments, quality thresholds, and contract terms are converted into explicit runtime prerequisites and escalation requirements.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment and future promotion to a retained-implementation package if demand justifies it.
